lofi.description: Lo-fi hip hop is defined by slow, mellow beats with intentional audio imperfections — vinyl crackle, tape hiss, background ambience. It is designed for studying, relaxing, or background listening. Tempo is typically 60-90 BPM.

ambient.description: Ambient music prioritizes atmosphere over rhythm or melody. It creates immersive soundscapes using synthesizers, drones, and field recordings. Very low energy, designed to blend into the background.

jazz.description: Jazz is characterized by improvisation, swing rhythms, and complex chord progressions. It ranges from upbeat bebop to slow ballads. Acoustic instruments — piano, bass, trumpet — dominate.

classical.description: Classical music spans centuries of orchestral, chamber, and solo instrumental composition. Ranges from gentle piano pieces to powerful symphonies. Almost entirely acoustic.

pop.description: Pop music is built for wide appeal — catchy hooks, polished production, and verse-chorus structure. High energy and danceability. Dominated by synthesizers and digital production.

indie pop.description: Indie pop blends pop sensibilities with lo-fi or alternative production aesthetics. Less polished than mainstream pop, more emotionally nuanced. Mid-range energy.

r&b.description: R&B (Rhythm and Blues) combines soulful vocals with groove-oriented production. Emotional, smooth, and often romantic. Mid-to-high energy with strong bass lines.

hip-hop.description: Hip-hop is built on drum machines, samples, and rap vocals. Ranges from laid-back boom-bap to aggressive trap. High energy and low acousticness are typical.

rock.description: Rock is defined by electric guitars, bass, and drums. Ranges from melodic alternative rock to hard rock and punk. Generally high energy with strong rhythm.

metal.description: Metal is characterized by heavily distorted guitars, fast drumming, and aggressive vocal delivery. Among the highest energy genres. Very low acousticness.

electronic.description: Electronic music is produced entirely with synthesizers, drum machines, and digital tools. Ranges from ambient techno to high-energy EDM. Very low acousticness.

synthwave.description: Synthwave is a retro-futuristic genre inspired by 1980s film soundtracks and video games. Lush synthesizers, driving drums, and nostalgic aesthetics. Mid-to-high energy.

country.description: Country music centers on storytelling, acoustic guitars, and emotional themes of love, loss, and rural life. Mid-range energy with high acousticness.
